readerTask: Explain the session-sharing problem, the shipped short-term flag, and the most useful next product decision without turning the four longer-term options into an unearned recommendation.
requiredFacts: Legacy cross-user spawn could borrow the parent owner's application identity and credentials.
The safe default attributes cross-user spawn to the caller.
The legacy flag does not override strict-mode Unix identity validation.
Longer-term options are sketches and unscheduled.
inferences: Real friction from the flag should inform which redesign option is worth pursuing.
limitations: The source does not establish demand, cost, or a winning redesign option.
decisionBoundary: Recommend what evidence/decision is needed next; do not select a roadmap option as settled fact.
prohibitedOverclaims: The legacy flag works in strict mode.
A longer-term option is approved or scheduled.
The flag removes all credential-sharing risk.

questions.q: What risk did the safe default remove?
questions.a: Unconditional cross-user identity borrowing on spawn/fork.

questions.q: Why does flag-on not restore legacy behavior in strict mode?
questions.a: The caller-stamped Unix identity conflicts with the parent-owner application identity and validation refuses execution.

questions.q: What is the next useful decision?
questions.a: Whether observed demand/friction justifies deeper redesign research, not which option is already chosen.
